content: read reviews/91.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/91.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/91.md.

    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos91 ... end Erdos91`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos91`).

    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `numDistinctDistances` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    30→- [x] `AreSimilar` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    31→- [x] `numDistinctDistances` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `Finset.image`, `Finset.filter`, `dist`).
    32→- [x] `AreSimilar` is a `Prop` (existential), does not need `noncomputable`.
    33→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    34→- [x] `AreSimilar` (a `Prop`) uses `UpperCamelCase`.
    35→- [x] `numDistinctDistances` (returns `ℕ`) uses `lowerCamelCase`.
    36→- [ ] No `local notation` needed (none used). N/A.
    37→- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. Optional but could be useful to verify `numDistinctDistances` and `AreSimilar` behave as expected on small examples.
    38→
    39→## 6. Theorem Statements
    40→
    41→- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
    42→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_91` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    43→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the same attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 52]`.
    44→- [x] Has a docstring referencing source papers `[Er87b, Er90, Er97e]`.
    45→
    46→### `answer()` usage
    47→
    48→The original problem asks: "is it true that every $n$-point set..." — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    49→
    50→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
    51→- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ N`, `∀ n`, `∀ A`, etc.) appear **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS of `↔`, not as theorem parameters.
    52→- [x] The RHS faithfully formalizes the question as asked: for sufficiently large $n$, every $n$-point minimizer of distinct distances has a non-similar minimizer of the same size.
    53→
    54→## 7. Category Attribute
    55→
    56→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute on the theorem.
    57→- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
    58→- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    59→
    60→## 8. AMS Attribute
    61→
    62→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    63→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `52`.
    64→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
    65→- [x] No duplicates.
    66→- [x] `52` (Convex and discrete geometry) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code and appropriate for a distinct-distances problem in the plane.
    67→
    68→## 9. Proof Bodies
    69→
    70→- [x] Open conjecture uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` — acceptable per checklist.
    71→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `structure` declarations.
    72→- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
    73→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    74→
    75→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    76→
    77→- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single conjecture.
    78→
    79→## 11. Code Quality
    80→
    81→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    82→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `≤`, `∀`, `∃`, `¬`, `↔`, `∧`, `≠`.
    83→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    84→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    85→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all appear under 100 characters).
    86→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    87→
    88→## 12. Build Verification
    89→
    90→- [ ] `lake build` not tested in this review.
    91→- [ ] Custom linter compliance not verified at build time.
    92→
    93→---
    94→
    95→## Summary
    96→
    97→**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)
    98→
    99→The file is clean and well-structured. All checklist items are satisfied:
   100→

   102→- Custom definitions (`numDistinctDistances`, `AreSimilar`) are properly documented, correctly named per convention, and appropriately marked `noncomputable` where needed.
   103→- The main theorem `erdos_91` correctly uses Case 1 (`answer(sorry) ↔ P`) since the original problem is a yes/no question ("is it true that..."). Quantifiers are correctly placed on the RHS.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/91.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 91`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `https://www.erdosproblems.com/91`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (uniqueness of distance-minimizing point sets up to similarity).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er87b]`, `[Er90]`, `[Er97e]`, `[Ko24c]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos91 ... end Erdos91`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos91`).
- [x] `open Finset Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `numDistinctDistances` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `AreSimilar` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `numDistinctDistances` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `Finset.image`, `Finset.filter`, `dist`).
- [x] `AreSimilar` is a `Prop` (existential), does not need `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `AreSimilar` (a `Prop`) uses `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] `numDistinctDistances` (returns `ℕ`) uses `lowerCamelCase`.
- [ ] No `local notation` needed (none used). N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. Optional but could be useful to verify `numDistinctDistances` and `AreSimilar` behave as expected on small examples.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_91` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the same attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 52]`.
- [x] Has a docstring referencing source papers `[Er87b, Er90, Er97e]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks: "is it true that every $n$-point set..." — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ N`, `∀ n`, `∀ A`, etc.) appear **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS of `↔`, not as theorem parameters.
- [x] The RHS faithfully formalizes the question as asked: for sufficiently large $n$, every $n$-point minimizer of distinct distances has a non-similar minimizer of the same size.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute on the theorem.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `52`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `52` (Convex and discrete geometry) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code and appropriate for a distinct-distances problem in the plane.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` — acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `≤`, `∀`, `∃`, `¬`, `↔`, `∧`, `≠`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all appear under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not tested in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter compliance not verified at build time.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All checklist items are satisfied:

- Copyright, import, module docstring, and namespace structure are all correct.
- Custom definitions (`numDistinctDistances`, `AreSimilar`) are properly documented, correctly named per convention, and appropriately marked `noncomputable` where needed.
- The main theorem `erdos_91` correctly uses Case 1 (`answer(sorry) ↔ P`) since the original problem is a yes/no question ("is it true that..."). Quantifiers are correctly placed on the RHS.
- Category (`research open`) and AMS (`52`) attributes are valid and on the same line.
- Code quality is good throughout with consistent formatting and appropriate use of Unicode.

No issues found.

content.text: The review passed with **Result: PASS**. No changes needed.
